title: Cataract review

referral.text: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.
referral.rationale: Supports cataract information, visual function, quality of life and referral discussion for adult cataract review; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.2 — cataract information, visual function, quality of life, and referral discussion

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Eye, symptoms, acuity, glare, night driving, function, quality of life, examination, comorbidity, diagnosis and referral discussion are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for adult cataract review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
